title: hymenophyllum rugosum
intro: Hymenophyllum rugosum, also known as the Hairy Forked Rock Fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to Australia and New Zealand. It is a vascular plant that belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
